author: coordinator-2
authorized_by: coordination-directive 2026-07-08 (fix the gaps F1 exposed + build the missing analyses); registrar acting as coordinator-2
changes: corpus pins: recipe replaced by kot-corpus-hash/1 with reproducible digest for lexical-wn31; unused kernel-v0/molecules-v0 pins and the never-built store-expansion-1e6 PINNED-AT-INPUTS placeholder removed (pins minimized to what the pinned instrument reads)

analysis contract: analysis/f1.py rewritten so its input contract exactly matches the pinned byte instrument's output keys (n_records, kernel_pack_bytes, gloss_zstd_bytes); the seed contract demanded store_scale + retrieval-latency fields no committed instrument measured, which made every future verdict-gen run crash and forced the seq-0 run to exploratory

endpoints: primary is the byte ratio the instrument measures (sum gloss_zstd_bytes / sum kernel_pack_bytes vs the pre-registered 2.0 line); the sec-latency endpoint and the latency verdict-rule leg are REMOVED from this record — the HE5 '<=2x retrieval latency' sub-premise is deferred to the F5 accuracy leg, where retrieval is actually served and a latency instrument can be pinned meaningfully; no committed/pinned on-box retrieval instrument exists on the 2-core box, and freezing an undecidable endpoint would leave the record permanently INCOMPLETE-DATA

store-scale IV (1e3/1e5/1e6) collapsed to the committed lexical-wn31 corpus (~1.18e5 records): the 1e3/1e6 rungs were never built and depended on an ops amendment the minimal- spine verdict-gen cannot process; the byte ratio is a per-record format property measured on the full committed corpus

coverage_requirement REMOVED (correction c-f1-2): the endpoint is a storage-format byte ratio over a fixed record set — model-free and task-free — so gating it on m0b task coverage is not scientifically apt and created a hard m0b-before-F1 ordering that P1 never declared (P1 orders m0b before FIRST EXTERNAL QUOTATION, not before F1). The NICHE-SCOPE discipline still binds any external quotation of the byte claim via the report layer, and m0b runs as its own Tier-0 experiment regardless
instrument + packer + zstd settings pinned in pins.harness_manifest
corpus_pin_defect: Corpus pins regenerated under kot-corpus-hash/1 (tools/registry/corpus-pin.py; recipe string embedded in pins.corpus_hashes._recipe). The GNG-0-seed digests did not reproduce under their own stated recipe in ~40 literal interpretations (pin-GENERATION defect surfaced by the F1 exploratory run seq 0, pins_observed block; corpus files were git-clean throughout — no data drift). prereg-freeze now recomputes every non-placeholder corpus digest at freeze time and refuses mismatches (ERR_P2_CORPUS_PIN); registry-check gained a --corpus-pins check.
kill_criterion_status: kill criterion verbatim text UNCHANGED (P1 section 3 HE5: byte claim dropped if <2x vs compressed gloss text); the byte bar is identical, and the record's scope note states it decides the byte premise only
legitimacy: Lawful pre-registration correction, NOT a post-hoc amendment: at correction time GNG-0 had NOT been maintainer-signed (frozen-index _meta records external timestamping as deferred) and the experiment's results-log contained NO phase:final run record (f1's log holds one exploratory-phase record only; m0b/g6/g7 have no logs). The P-9 design-amendment cutoff (first final-phase run) was therefore not crossed for any corrected record. The provisional freeze was reset to DRAFT, corrected, and re-frozen through prereg-freeze under the same tooling lints plus the new freeze-time corpus-pin verification.
